questionHtml:
A hunter tends to kill the bigger individuals of a population for their meat or for large ornamental trophies. Therefore, the population tends to have more individuals who are smaller. What is this an example of?

A. Directional selection

B. Disruptive selection

C. Natural selection

D. Stabilizing selection

meta.title: A hunter tends to kill the bigger individuals of a population for their meat or for large ornamental trophies. Therefore, the population tends to have more individuals who are smal
